sourceMovie.title: Pan
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Pan (2015) as a fantasy theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The fantasy and adventure blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Maleficent
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Pan clicked for you, Maleficent (2014) is a strong next stop: both trade in fantasy and adventure, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: How to Train Your Dragon
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Pan clicked for you, How to Train Your Dragon (2010) is a strong next stop: both trade in fantasy and adventure, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: The Chronicles of Narnia: The Voyage of the Dawn Treader
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Pan clicked for you, The Chronicles of Narnia: The Voyage of the Dawn Treader (2010) is a strong next stop: both trade in fantasy and adventure, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Pan?
faq.answer: Try Maleficent, How to Train Your Dragon, The Chronicles of Narnia: The Voyage of the Dawn Treader—each shares DNA with Pan while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Pan?
faq.answer: Pan (2015) is best described as Fantasy, Adventure, Family, Action. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Pan worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this fantasy lane.
